```cpp
// add+modify_eas_records.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <map>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "eas_common.hpp"
using namespace sqlgen;
using namespace sqlgen::literals;

template<typename T>
void add_eas_records(const std::string& table_name,
                     const std::vector<T>& records) {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path);
  if (!conn_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to database");
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Get the field names of T
  using NamedTupleType = rfl::named_tuple_t<std::remove_cvref_t<T>>;
  using Fields = typename NamedTupleType::Fields;

  // Build the CREATE TABLE statement
  std::string create_table_sql = "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS " + table_name + " (";
  constexpr int num_fields = rfl::tuple_size_v<Fields>;
  for (int i = 0; i < num_fields; ++i) {
    using FieldType = rfl::tuple_element_t<i, Fields>;
    using OriginalType = typename FieldType::Type;
    using Name = typename FieldType::Name;
    create_table_sql += Name().str() + " ";
    // We don't know the type, so we will use TEXT for simplicity
    create_table_sql += "TEXT";
    if (i < num_fields - 1) {
      create_table_sql += ", ";
    } else {
      create_table_sql += ")";
    }
  }

  // Execute the CREATE TABLE statement
  auto create_result = conn->execute(create_table_sql);
  if (!create_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create table");
  }

  // Build and execute the INSERT statements
  for (const auto& record : records) {
    std::string insert_sql = "INSERT INTO " + table_name + " VALUES (";
    for (int i = 0; i < num_fields; ++i) {
      if (i != 0) {
        insert_sql += ", ";
      }
      insert_sql += "?";
    }
    insert_sql += ")";

    // We need to convert the record to a vector of optional strings
    std::vector<std::optional<std::string>> values(num_fields);
    for (int i = 0; i < num_fields; ++i) {
      using FieldType = rfl::tuple_element_t<i, Fields>;
      using OriginalType = typename FieldType::Type;
      using Name = typename FieldType::Name;
      auto value = rfl::get<Name>(record);
      if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, sqlgen::PrimaryKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>>) ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, sqlgen::Varchar<30>) ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_v<OriginalType>, std::string>) {
        values[i] = value.value();
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, int> ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, uint32_t> ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, double>) {
        values[i] = std::to_string(value.value());
      } else if constexpr (std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime>) ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, std::optional<uint32_t>) ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, std::optional<double>) ||
                    std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<OriginalType>, std::optional<std::string>) {
        if (value) {
          values[i] = value.value();
        } else {
          values[i] = std::nullopt;
        }
      } else {
        static_assert(rfl::always_false_v<OriginalType>, "Unsupported type");
      }
    }

    // Execute the INSERT statement
    auto insert_result = conn->insert(insert_sql, values.begin(), values.end());
    if (!insert_result) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Failed to insert record");
    }
  }
}

void delete_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& table_name,
                               const std::string& record_id) {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path);
  if (!conn_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to database");
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Build the DELETE statement
  std::string delete_sql = "DELETE FROM " + table_name + " WHERE id = ?";

  // Execute the DELETE statement
  auto delete_result = conn->execute(delete_sql, {record_id});
  if (!delete_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to delete record");
  }
}
```

          }

          // Build the query
          auto query = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Book>> | sqlgen::where(condition);

#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
  design_eas_database_with_major();
  
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();
  
  // Add Grade column to Score table
  (sqlgen::alter_table<Score> | sqlgen::add_column<"Grade", sqlgen::Varchar<1>>)(conn).value();
  
  // Modify Age column in Student table to int (allowing nulls)
  (sqlgen::alter_table<Student> | sqlgen::modify_column<"Age", int>)(conn).value();
  
  // Drop Major table
  (sqlgen::drop<Major> | sqlgen::if_exists)(conn).value();
}

} // namespace update_eas_table
```</answer>

    #include <sqlgen.hpp>

    namespace calculate_order_metrics {

    struct OrderItem {
      sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
      int order_id;
      int product_id;
      double unit_price;
      int quantity;
      std::optional<double> discount_rate;
      std::optional<double> tax_rate;
    };

    struct OrderMetrics {
      int item_id;
      int order_id;
      int product_id;
      double unit_price;
      int quantity;
      double discount_rate;
      double tax_rate;
      double gross_total;
      double discounted_total;
      double final_total;
      double unit_cost;
      int quantity_group;
      double estimated_profit;
    };

    std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
      // Connect to the database
      auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();

      // Drop the table if it exists
      auto drop_table = sqlgen::drop<OrderItem> | sqlgen::if_exists;
      drop_table(conn).value();

      // Write the data to the table
      sqlgen::write(conn, items.begin(), items.end()).value();

      // Build the query
      auto query = sqlgen::select_from<OrderItem>(
          "item_id"_c, 
          "order_id"_c, 
          "product_id"_c, 
          "unit_price"_c, 
          "quantity"_c, 
          "discount_rate"_c, 
          "tax_rate"_c,
          ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c).as<"gross_total">(),
          ( ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - (sqlgen::coalesce("discount_rate"_c, 0.0) / 100)) ).as<"discounted_total">(),
          ( ( ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - (sqlgen::coalesce("discount_rate"_c, 0.0) / 100)) ) * (1 + (sqlgen::coalesce("tax_rate"_c, 0.0) / 100)) ).as<"final_total">(),
          ( ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) / "quantity"_c ).as<"unit_cost">(),
          ("quantity"_c % 10).as<"quantity_group">(),
          ("unit_price"_c * 0.3).as<"estimated_profit">()
      );

      // Execute the query and map to OrderMetrics
      auto result = (query | sqlgen::to<std::vector<OrderMetrics>>)(conn).value();

      // Drop the table
      drop_table(conn).value();

      return result;
    }

    }  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    updated_notes.push_back(UpdatedNote{note.note_id, note.content});
  }

  return updated_notes;
}

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace drop_table_if_exists {

struct Log {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string message;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates dropping a table if it exists.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string drop_table_if_exists() {
    auto drop_stmt = sqlgen::drop<Log> | sqlgen::if_exists;
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(drop_stmt);
}

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists
```</answer>

```cpp
std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Read the joined table of Project and TimeLog
  auto joined_rows = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<rfl::NamedTuple<...>>> 
                      | sqlgen::inner_join<Project, TimeLog>(/* condition */))(*conn);

  // Transform each joined row into a TimeMetrics object
  std::vector<TimeMetrics> metrics;
  for (const auto& row : joined_rows) {
    // Extract the project_id and name from the Project part of the row
    int project_id = row.project_id;
    std::string name = row.name;

    // Extract the start_time and end_time from the TimeLog part of the row
    auto start_time = row.start_time;
    auto end_time = row.end_time;

    // Use sqlgen's temporal operations to extract day, month, year, hour, minute, second from start_time
    int day = sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::Day, start_time);
    int month = sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::Month, start_time);
    int year = sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::Year, start_time);
    int hour = sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::Hour, start_time);
    int minute = sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::Minute, start_time);
    int second = sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::Second, start_time);

    // Compute the days between start_time and end_time
    double duration_days = sqlgen::days_between(start_time, end_time);

    // Create the TimeMetrics object
    TimeMetrics metric{
        .project_id = project_id,
        .name = name,
        .day = day,
        .month = month,
        .year = year,
        .hour = hour,
        .minute = minute,
        .second = second,
        .duration_days = duration_days
    };

    metrics.push_back(metric);
  }

  return metrics;
}
```
